headline: Lebanese Presidency, citing Lebanese embassy in the US: Hezbollah agreed to the US proposal on mutual cessation of attacks that would be expanded to all Lebanese territory.
Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah: Hezbollah had refused a partial truce offer to spare Beirut from Israeli strikes if Hezbollah stopped attacks on northern Israel.
Lebanon's efforts, together with the US administration, have yielded a breakthrough in terms of achieving a ceasefire - Kan News Reporter, citing sources close to the Lebanese Prime Minister
We won’t attack Beirut, but we won’t withdraw our troops from their current positions either - Al Hadath, citing an Israeli Official.
